title: A Visit to the Clinic
learningGoal: The learner will decode narrative medical contexts, identifying symptom description syntax ('I have a...') and standard clinical process vocabulary (prescription, pharmacy).
instructions: Read the story about Amit's bad day. He is very sick! Notice how he describes his pain to the doctor. He never uses the word 'Paining'!

contentItems.text: Pay close attention to what Amit receives from the doctor, and where he has to go to buy his medicine.

readingText: Amit wakes up in the morning and feels terrible. He has a headache and a very bad stomachache. He cannot go to work today. He calls his office and says, 'I am sick. I need to see a doctor.' Amit drives to the hospital. The nurse asks him, 'What is wrong?' Amit answers, 'I have a fever, and my stomach hurts.' The doctor examines him and smiles. 'Do not worry,' the doctor says. 'You just have the flu. You need to rest and drink plenty of water.' The doctor gives Amit a prescription. Amit takes the prescription to the pharmacy inside the hospital. He buys his medicine and goes home to sleep.
textInTargetLanguage: Amit wakes up in the morning and feels terrible. He has a headache and a very bad stomachache. He cannot go to work today. He calls his office and says, 'I am sick. I need to see a doctor.' Amit drives to the hospital. The nurse asks him, 'What is wrong?' Amit answers, 'I have a fever, and my stomach hurts.' The doctor examines him and smiles. 'Do not worry,' the doctor says. 'You just have the flu. You need to rest and drink plenty of water.' The doctor gives Amit a prescription. Amit takes the prescription to the pharmacy inside the hospital. He buys his medicine and goes home to sleep.
baseLanguageSupportText: अमित सुबह उठता है और बहुत बुरा महसूस करता है। उसे सिरदर्द और बहुत तेज पेट दर्द है। वह आज काम पर नहीं जा सकता। वह अपने ऑफिस फोन करता है और कहता है, 'मैं बीमार हूँ। मुझे डॉक्टर को दिखाने की ज़रूरत है।' अमित कार चलाकर अस्पताल जाता है। नर्स उससे पूछती है, 'क्या तकलीफ है?' अमित जवाब देता है, 'मुझे बुखार है, और मेरे पेट में दर्द है (मेरा पेट दर्द कर रहा है)।' डॉक्टर उसकी जांच करता है और मुस्कुराता है। 'चिंता मत करो,' डॉक्टर कहता है। 'तुम्हें बस फ्लू है। तुम्हें आराम करने और खूब पानी पीने की जरूरत है।' डॉक्टर अमित को एक पर्चा (प्रिस्क्रिप्शन) देता है। अमित पर्चे को अस्पताल के अंदर बने दवाखाने (फार्मेसी) में ले जाता है। वह अपनी दवा खरीदता है और सोने के लिए घर चला जाता है।

exampleSentence.targetText: I cannot read because I have a headache.
exampleSentence.transliteration: आई कैनॉट रीड बिकॉज़ आई हैव अ हेडेक।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: मैं पढ़ नहीं सकता क्योंकि मेरे सिर में दर्द है।

exampleSentence.targetText: If you are sick, you must see a doctor.
exampleSentence.transliteration: इफ यू आर सिक, यू मस्ट सी अ डॉक्टर।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: यदि आप बीमार हैं, तो आपको डॉक्टर को दिखाना चाहिए।
आज मेरे हाथ में दर्द है।

exampleSentence.targetText: Take this prescription to the pharmacy.
exampleSentence.transliteration: टेक दिस प्रिस्क्रिप्शन टू द फार्मेसी।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: इस पर्चे को दवाखाने ले जाओ।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: Amit wakes up in the morning and feels terrible.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: अमित वेक्स अप इन द मॉर्निंग एंड फील्स टेरिबल।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: अमित सुबह उठता है और बहुत बुरा महसूस करता है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: I have a fever, and my stomach hurts.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: आई हैव अ फीवर, एंड माई स्टमक हर्ट्स।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: मुझे बुखार है, और मेरे पेट में दर्द है।

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: The doctor gives Amit a prescription.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: द डॉक्टर गिव्स अमित अ प्रिस्क्रिप्शन।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: डॉक्टर अमित को एक पर्चा देता है।

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Why cannot Amit go to work today?
What exact phrase does Amit use when talking to his office?
options: I need to see a doctor.

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: True or False: Amit says 'My stomach is paining' to the nurse.
What verb does Amit use to describe his stomach pain?
What disease does the doctor say Amit has? (He has the ____).
The doctor gives him a piece of paper. What is the English word for this paper?
Where does Amit take the piece of paper to get his medicine?
Fill in the blank from the story: The doctor tells him to rest and drink plenty of ____.

correctAnswers.q2: I need to see a doctor.

expectedSummaryPoints: Learner correctly identifies 'hurts' as the proper verb for localized pain, recognizing the absence of 'is paining'.
Learner correctly associates 'prescription' with 'pharmacy' in the medical workflow.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You perfectly understood Amit's hospital visit! You noticed how he used 'hurts' instead of 'is paining', and you learned the workflow of taking a Prescription to the Pharmacy.
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, Amit NEVER said 'My stomach is paining'. He correctly said 'My stomach hurts'. Read the story again and look for where he gets the paper for his medicine (Prescription).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for Question 6. A 'Receipt' is what you get after you buy something at a store. But a paper from a doctor is called a 'Prescription'!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Amit wakes up in the morning and feels terrible... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Read the conversation with the nurse carefully. What exactly does Amit tell her is wrong with his stomach?
commonMistakes: Thinking a prescription is the same as medicine, rather than the permission slip for the medicine.
